Summary: [F-D01] Tools registered with @mcp.tool() but missing from tool_tiers.py
Description: Pass 1 audit: some MCP tools are decorated with @mcp.tool() but have no entry in mcp/core/tool_tiers.py. These tools default-deny silently when callers reach them. Add an entry to tool_tiers.py for every registered tool. CI gate test_consistency_gates.py::TestToolTiers prevents regression. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D02] Cerbos resource_mcp_tool.yaml has duplicate or multi-tier tool entries
Description: Some tools appear in multiple tier rules in mcp/auth/policies/resource_mcp_tool.yaml; some appear duplicated within the same tier. Dedupe; ensure each tool appears in exactly one tier. CI gate TestCerbosPolicy prevents regression. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D03] Phantom tool names referenced in requirements/*.md docs
Description: Requirements docs reference tool names that aren't actually registered. Either register the tools, fix the docs, or add to tests/ci/requirements_doc_allowlist.txt with a comment. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D04] Confidence scoring 7-signal documentation drift
Description: docs/architecture/review-gate.md does not match the canonical 7-signal scoring (base 20). Update doc to match implementation. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D05] PatternStore documentation says Neo4j+Qdrant but implementation is Qdrant-only
Description: Per user constitution, PatternStore is Qdrant-only. Drop the neo4j_driver= kwarg from PatternStore constructor (already done in W-07). Update docs. See workstream W-07.

Summary: [F-D06] Tool count claim drift across docs (62 vs 56 vs others)
Description: Multiple docs claim different MCP tool counts. Run `python -c 'from mcp.core.mcp_server import mcp; print(len(mcp._tools))'` to get the actual count and update docs/DOCUMENTATION.md, docs/architecture/OVERVIEW.md, README.md. CI gate TestToolCount prevents regression. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D07] DA list count drift (21 vs 21+ vs 22 across docs)
Description: Different docs claim 21 / 21+ / 22 DAs. Pick the canonical number based on DA_TASK_MAPPING in rlm_orchestrator.py. Update docs. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D08] MCP_QUICKSTART.md sample code uses outdated tool argument name
Description: Sample code in docs/MCP_QUICKSTART.md uses old parameter names that don't match current tool signatures. Audit and update. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D09] Storage config docs don't match storage_config.yaml fields
Description: Documentation references config fields that don't exist or omits fields that do exist. Reconcile. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D10] Ontology profile names drift between docs and ontology.yaml
Description: Docs reference profile names that don't match the actual profile keys in config/ontology.yaml. Reconcile. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D11] tool_tiers.py role names don't match Cerbos role definitions
Description: tool_tiers.py uses role labels that differ slightly from the role hierarchy in mcp/auth/policies/derived_roles.yaml. Pick canonical names; update consistently. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D12] Sprint version markers in code comments don't reflect current Sprint
Description: Many files have 'Sprint 4-5' or 'Sprint 8' markers in docstrings. After the cleanup work, update to reflect current state or remove. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D13] TODO comments referencing closed JIRA tickets
Description: Several TODO comments in code reference JIRA tickets that have been closed but the TODO was never removed. Audit and remove. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D14] Tool count discrepancy in docs/DOCUMENTATION.md vs README
Description: Same as F-D06 from a different angle. Resolve in single pass. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D15] API path examples in docs use http:// instead of https://
Description: MCP_QUICKSTART.md sample URLs use http://. Update to https://. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D16] Module list in docs/DOCUMENTATION.md (ADC, CAN, ...) outdated
Description: iLLD module list claims '~12 modules (ADC, SPI, CAN, CXPI, etc.)' — actual count from ontology may differ. Reconcile. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D17] Sprint version numbers in docstrings inconsistent
Description: Some files declare 'Sprint 8 (current)' which is stale. Bulk-update or remove. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D18] Architecture diagrams in OVERVIEW.md show 13 categories; reality is 14
Description: Box-and-arrow diagrams enumerate 13 tool categories; current categorization has 14 (with GAP added). Update diagram. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D19] AI_USAGE_POLICY.md references DA codes that don't match RLM mapping
Description: Policy doc uses 'CIA (F2)' and 'CIA (F3)' for sub-features; RLM doesn't model these. Either model them or remove from policy. See workstream W-15.

Summary: [F-D20] Sample env vars in docs differ from .env.template defaults
Description: MCP_QUICKSTART.md and docs/DEPLOYMENT.md show env vars (GPT4IFX_BASE_URL, etc.) with values that differ from .env.template. Reconcile. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D21] Workspace name 'illd' vs 'iLLD' inconsistent across docs
Description: Some docs use 'illd' (lowercase, the actual workspace ID) and others use 'iLLD' (the marketing form). Lowercase 'illd' is canonical for workspace IDs; reserve 'iLLD' for prose mentions of the product. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D22] Confusion between MCAL workspace 'mcal' and module names like 'Adc'
Description: Some docs conflate workspace and module. Add a glossary section in docs/DOCUMENTATION.md clarifying the two-level scope. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-D23] MISRA C version references mix 2012 and 2023
Description: User confirmed iLLD has adopted MISRA C:2023; some docs still reference 2012. Audit and update where appropriate. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-A01] Filesystem casing collision: src/IngestionPipeline/Parsers/ vs parsers/
Description: Both 'Parsers/' and 'parsers/' exist in IngestionPipeline. On case-insensitive filesystems (macOS default, Windows) they collapse; on Linux container deployment they remain separate, breaking imports. Choose one casing (lowercase 'parsers/' is Python convention) and migrate. CI gate gates:filesystem-case prevents regression. See workstream W-22 + W-23.

Summary: [F-A02] swa_parsers.py duplicated byte-equivalent across IngestionPipeline/Parsers/ and HybridRAG/code/KG/
Description: Two byte-equivalent copies of swa_parsers.py exist. Pick one location (IngestionPipeline/Parsers/ is the right home), delete the other, update imports. See workstream W-17.

Summary: [F-A03] ContextBuilder duality: HybridRAG (Sprint 8) vs MemoryLayer (Sprint 2)
Description: ContextBuilder exists in two places. The Sprint 8 HybridRAG version is canonical; the MemoryLayer version is legacy. Move canonical to MemoryLayer (architecturally correct), rename legacy to LegacyContextBuilder, re-export from HybridRAG. See workstream W-18.

Summary: [F-A04] mcp_server.py is ~1800 lines; should be split into aice_mcp/tools/ subpackage
Description: Single 1800-line file is hard to navigate. Split into aice_mcp/tools/{search,kg_query,traceability,...}.py per category. Main file imports + registers. Deferred to Sprint 30 architectural sprint. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A05] ILLD KG builders live in HybridRAG/code/KG; architecturally belong in IngestionPipeline
Description: Per Pass 2 §5 target, KG builders should be in IngestionPipeline/KG/, not HybridRAG/code/KG/. HybridRAG should only consume the KG, not build it. Sprint 30 architectural sprint. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A06] FeedbackSink and ReviewGate concerns intermixed in confidence.py
Description: Single file holds both confidence scoring (read-only) and feedback persistence (write-side). Split into review_gate/confidence.py (scoring) and review_gate/feedback_sink.py (persistence). See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A07] Legacy query_knowledge_graph.py (1595 LoC) coexists with newer KG query path
Description: Old 'Legacy' file remains in tree alongside newer query path. Delete or feature-flag. Decision: delete in Sprint 30. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A08] RLM orchestrator imports from HybridRAG; should be in MemoryLayer
Description: Per Pass 2 §5, RLM is a Memory & Context concern. Currently lives in HybridRAG/code/querier/. Move to MemoryLayer/memory/rlm/. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A09] No layer-dependency contracts enforced via import-linter
Description: Pass 2 §5 target architecture has clean layer rules but they're not mechanically enforced. Add .import-linter.toml (already specified in Pass 4b §8). See workstream W-18.

Summary: [F-A10] mcp/ shadows the FastMCP SDK package name
Description: AICE's 'mcp/' directory has the same name as the FastMCP Python SDK. Rename to 'aice_mcp/' to disambiguate. Single repo-wide sweep. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A11] Cross-cutting concerns scattered: config loading in 4+ places
Description: env_config, storage_config, ontology config — loaders scattered. Consolidate in src/Configuration/. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A12] Observability primitives mixed into business logic
Description: Prometheus metric definitions live alongside business code. Move to src/Observability/metrics.py with explicit registration. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A13] auth_middleware.py mixes ASGI middleware + key registry + Cerbos client
Description: Three distinct responsibilities in one module. Split into asgi/, registry/, cerbos/ submodules. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A14] Connector classes vary in patterns (some take config dict, some kwargs)
Description: Inconsistent constructor signatures across Jama/Polarion/Jenkins/Bitbucket. Standardize. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-A15] ServiceState pattern not used consistently for non-MCP services
Description: ServiceState (W-07) covers MCP-tool services but ingestion service helpers don't use it. Extend. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-A16] Test directory structure doesn't mirror source tree
Description: tests/ flat layout doesn't reflect the layered architecture. Restructure tests/ to mirror src/ for navigation. See workstream W-26.

Summary: [F-CA-A01] API key registry cached forever; no reload path
Description: _api_key_registry is loaded once at module import; reload_api_keys() is dead code; no apscheduler wiring. Under Gunicorn, key rotation has unpredictable propagation. Add apscheduler-driven reload every 60s + mtime check. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A02] _none placeholder role pattern is fragile
Description: auth_middleware.resolve_principal returns a placeholder role '_none' instead of None when a workspace is missing roles. One accidental edit to derived_roles.yaml making _none a parent of any real role flips everything to allow-all. Replace with proper Optional[Principal] return. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A03] Cerbos DENIES not written to PostgreSQL audit_logs
Description: check_authorization logs DENIES to stderr only. Compliance-relevant access denials are lost on log rotation. Write DENIES to audit_logs with authorization='denied'. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A04] Cerbos PDP unreachable falls back to local-tier silently; no timeout
Description: When Cerbos network call hangs or fails, behavior depends on httpx defaults — could hang forever. Add 1s timeout, reconnect-on-failure, Prometheus gauge aice_cerbos_up. See workstream W-07.

Summary: [F-CA-A05] API key partial-prefix labeled 'hash' in audit log
Description: auth_middleware uses api_key[:8]+'…' in audit rows, labeled as a hash. For short keys ('key-cia-001' = 11 chars), 8 chars = full structure. Replace with hashlib.sha256(api_key)[:16]. Add PostgreSQL migration to rename column. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A06] API keys loaded synchronously at module import (blocks ASGI startup)
Description: load_api_keys() does file I/O at import time. Under uvicorn, this delays startup. Move to lifespan handler. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A07] Missing api_keys.yaml results in WARNING + empty registry, not refusal-to-start
Description: If MCP_REQUIRE_AUTH=1 and api_keys.yaml missing/empty, system silently allows unauthenticated traffic. Add assert_auth_ready() at startup. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A08] _authorize doesn't propagate workspace context to Cerbos as resource attribute
Description: Cerbos policies that depend on workspace.attr.tier never see it. Pass workspace context. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-A09] API key entries in api_keys.yaml have no expiration
Description: Keys never expire. Add 'expires_at' field; refuse expired keys. See workstream W-08.

Summary: [F-CA-S01] hybrid_search() (sync) and hybrid_search_async() coexist
Description: Two parallel implementations of the same function. Pattern #3. Pick one (async is preferable for ASGI); deprecate the other. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-S02] _merge_results_rrf rename did not propagate from Master Gaps REV3-H01
Description: Master Gaps says renamed to _merge_results_weighted. Source still has old name. CI gate TestREV3H01_RRFRename catches this. Rename. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-S03] Token estimation uses // 3 in one path and // 4 in another
Description: Two distinct token-estimation impls in the search service. Consolidate to a single estimate_tokens helper. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-S04] Vector search availability not surfaced; sentence-transformers ImportError silently degrades
Description: If sentence-transformers not installed, _embed_query falls through to keyword-only search with no warning. Add aice_vector_search_available gauge. See workstream W-07.

Summary: [F-CA-S05] Search service has no negative caching of query failures
Description: Repeated failed searches retry the same backend each time. Add brief negative cache. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-S06] Search response shape varies between sync and async paths
Description: Subtle field differences confuse downstream consumers. Reconcile in W-CA-S01 fix. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-S07] alpha=0.5 hardcoded as fallback; should be config-driven
Description: Default RRF blend factor is hardcoded. Move to storage_config.yaml. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-I01] ingest_file accepts arbitrary paths from admin caller (path traversal + env exfil)
Description: ingest_file can be called with /proc/self/environ; contents land in the KG; subsequent search exposes GPT4IFX_PASSWORD, IFX_PASSWORD, etc. Combined with F-CF-X01 this is a complete IFX SSO compromise path. Add INGEST_ALLOWED_ROOTS allowlist + extension validation. See workstream W-05.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed in feature/sprint26-robustness-hardening (2026-06-19); ingest_file always calls safe_path_under with default roots /data,/repos (INGEST_ALLOWED_ROOTS override); permissive reject_traversal fallback removed; out-of-root supported files now rejected; verified by repro + tests.

Summary: [F-CA-I02] _discover_module_files silently caps at 100 files
Description: Module ingestion may silently miss files when the module exceeds 100 files. No warning. Add configurable cap, warn loudly when hit. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-I03] Ingestion job tracker doesn't record cause of partial failures
Description: When 47 of 50 files succeed, job marked 'completed' with no record of which 3 failed and why. Surface per-file failures. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-I04] ingest_repository discovers ALL modules across both workspaces if workspace= mismatched
Description: If workspace='illd' but repo_path contains MCAL modules, they get ingested into illd. Validate workspace against discovered modules. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-I05] Post-ingest callbacks (cache invalidation, metric emission) silent on failure
Description: If cache invalidation fails after ingestion, KG and cache are inconsistent. Add aice_post_ingest_callback_failures_total counter; surface in dashboard. See workstream W-12.

Summary: [F-CA-I06] ingest_module_from_repo doesn't validate module exists in repo
Description: Calling with module='Frobnicator' silently produces empty job. Validate. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-C01] save_feedback() doesn't pre-insert response_archive row → FK violation → silent loss
Description: When submit_human_feedback fires before evaluate_confidence has persisted the response_archive row, FK violation aborts the feedback insert. Per ASPICE SUP.10, every feedback record must persist. Add pre-insert idempotent upsert of response_archive. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-C02] Confidence signal weights hardcoded; not config-driven
Description: Eight signal weights live in code constants. Move to confidence_config.yaml so SQA can tune. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-C03] evaluate_confidence treats response as single object; ACRA/MIRA emit findings lists
Description: ReviewGate single-response model doesn't fit findings-list responses. Add evaluate_confidence_multi variant. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CA-C04] FeedbackSink wiring uses neo4j_driver= in some places, not in others
Description: Pass 1 F-D05 + W-07 fix. Drop neo4j_driver= entirely (PatternStore is Qdrant-only).

Summary: [F-CA-C05] Confidence score persisted as float without precision documentation
Description: PostgreSQL column is REAL; rounding behavior at 0.05 boundaries undocumented. Document or use NUMERIC(5,2). See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-01] TraceabilityPuller Cypher uses parameterized variable-length path; SyntaxError swallowed
Description: MATCH path=(n)-[*1..$depth]-(neighbor) — Neo4j does not support parameterized variable-length path bounds. Always raises SyntaxError. The try/except Exception swallows it; sandbox_upload returns prod_nodes_loaded:0 with success envelope. iLLD sandbox feature silently dead since Sprint 4-5. Inline depth as literal int. See workstream W-01.

Summary: [F-CB-02] Lazy-init pattern returns None on failure; no negative caching, no metric, no surfacing
Description: 12+ _get_* helpers in mcp_server.py share the antipattern. Every tool returns generic INTERNAL_ERROR with no clue. Replace with _ServiceState helper that caches failures, exposes via Prometheus gauge. See workstream W-07.

Summary: [F-CB-03] Neo4j driver constructed without verify_connectivity()
Description: First call surfaces connection failure on session.run, not at construction. Add verify_connectivity() in factory. See workstream W-07.

Summary: [F-CB-04] No correlation_id linking auth decision to tool execution to audit row
Description: Cannot join MCP request to its audit trail. Compliance audit becomes 'did this call succeed?' with no answer. Generate uuid4() at request start; propagate via contextvars. See workstream W-13.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): W-13 2026-06-19: _ensure_correlation_id() seeds _current_request_id in _authorize (covers stdio, not just HTTP middleware); correlation_id flows into _ok + _err envelopes AND audit_logs (NEW correlation_id column + log_audit param). Also fixed silently-dropped caller_api_key kwarg (log_audit had no such param -> audit writes were failing in try/except: pass). tests/unit/test_w13_typed_errors.py.

Summary: [F-CB-05] _tool_name_ctx may not be set; central Prometheus tool metric silently no-op
Description: Verify _tool_name_ctx.set() happens. If not, the central tool-metric is silently no-op. Wrap every @mcp.tool() with _instrument decorator. See workstream W-11.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Verified resolved 2026-06-19: _tool_name_ctx.set(tool_name) runs in _authorize (mcp_server.py) and _finish_tool records aice_tool_requests_total/_duration_seconds with the da_name label in _ok/_err. Metric is NOT a no-op. (Pass 5 tier label + aice_da_* productivity catalog still pending under F-P5-M01.)

Summary: [F-CB-06] Lifespan handler doesn't call _warmup
Description: Sprint 9 added _warmup but lifespan handler doesn't await it. First cold call pays full init cost. Add to lifespan. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-07] Tool category counts in mcp_server.py docstring drift from reality
Description: Module docstring claims '13 categories'; current is 14. Update or compute from registry. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-CB-08] _ok/_err envelopes have generic INTERNAL_ERROR; lose typed context
Description: Tool exceptions get wrapped as INTERNAL_ERROR. SREs cannot tell BackendUnavailable from BadInput. Add _err_from_exc(exc, component) helper with typed codes. See workstream W-13.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): W-13/F-CB-08 2026-06-19: _err_from_exc(exc, component) helper (typed codes, re-raises CancelledError, no infra leak) + central safety-net in with_session_routing + retrofitted all 59 generic _err(INTERNAL_ERROR, str(e|exc)) handler sites to function-aware _err_from_exc(...). Regression-guarded by tests/integration/test_sprint27_artifacts.py.

Summary: [F-CB-09] sandbox_upload writes to /tmp/sandbox_{session_id} without containment
Description: session_id is user-controlled; path traversal possible. shutil.rmtree(ignore_errors=True) on session_end can delete arbitrary directories. Add SANDBOX_TMP_ROOT containment + session_id validation. See workstream W-05.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed (2026-06-19); validate_session_id enforced at session_start, sandbox_upload and session_end before any /tmp/sandbox_{session_id} filesystem use.

Summary: [F-CB-10] _detect_module_from_names returns 'Std' or 'IfxCan' instead of real iLLD module
Description: Heuristic strips after first underscore but doesn't strip 'Ifx' prefix or skip 'Std' tokens. Combined with F-CB-01, double reason iLLD sandbox returns zero prod nodes. Fix regex with anchored re.sub. See workstream W-02.

Summary: [F-CB-11] _warmup is sequential despite Master Gaps PERF-02 claiming asyncio.gather
Description: Master Gaps says parallel; source is sequential for-loop. CI gate TestPERF02_ParallelWarmup catches. Use asyncio.gather. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-12] ASGI middleware doesn't propagate trace headers (X-Request-ID)
Description: If upstream proxy sends X-Request-ID, AICE doesn't honor it. Use as correlation_id when present. See workstream W-13.

Summary: [F-CB-13] MCP error messages contain stack traces in production
Description: _err_from_exc returns str(exc) which can include filesystem paths. Sanitize. See workstream W-13.

Summary: [F-CB-14] Async tool handlers may not respect cancellation
Description: asyncio.CancelledError caught and wrapped as INTERNAL_ERROR. Re-raise. See workstream W-13.

Summary: [F-CB-15] session_start TTL upper bound not enforced
Description: Caller can request ttl_seconds=10000000 and overflow Redis. Cap at 86400 (1 day). See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-16] Debug-level logs include API key fragments
Description: Even with F-CA-A05 fix, debug logs sometimes echo Authorization headers. Audit. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-17] PatternStore neo4j_driver= wiring buried in nested except; learning loop disabled silently since Sprint 9
Description: FeedbackSink → PatternStore wiring fails silently because PatternStore() raises on invalid neo4j_driver=. Per F-D05, drop the kwarg entirely. Result: ApprovedPattern nodes have not been written for many sprints. See workstream W-07.

Summary: [F-CB-18] _ok/_err envelopes lose context after _instrument decorator timing wrap
Description: Combined with W-11 timing wrapper, the response is finalized before metric emission. Re-order. See workstream W-11.

Summary: [F-CB-19] @mcp.tool() decorated functions don't have signature validation
Description: Function signatures may drift from declared MCP tool schema. Add runtime validation. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-20] _get_qdrant doesn't pin client API version
Description: Qdrant client major version bumps could break silently. Pin in requirements + assert version. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-21] session_start creates session in Redis without TTL when caller omits ttl_seconds
Description: Default of 3600 is in MCP tool definition but Redis ttl path may be missed. Verify. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-22] RLM tools (rlm_orchestrate, rlm_plan_preview) timeouts not configurable
Description: Long RLM runs can exceed FastMCP default timeout. Add per-tool timeout config. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-23] Visualization tool (Cat 12) emits matplotlib SVG without size cap
Description: Large graph visualizations can exceed response size limit. Cap node count. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CB-24] Health-check tool doesn't include cluster info (Neo4j readers/writers)
Description: Single 'up/down' obscures cluster health. Add cluster status if applicable. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-R01] _default_llm has no retry despite Master Gaps REV3-H03 claim
Description: Single try/except, single LLM attempt, no retry. ~7% RLM failure rate from network blips alone. Master Gaps says 3-attempt exponential backoff was added in REV3-H03; source disagrees. CI gate TestREV3H03_GPT4IFXRetry catches. See workstream W-09.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): OPEN (2026-06-19); W-09 added a retry decorator but it only catches builtin TimeoutError/ConnectionError - real OpenAI SDK errors (APIConnectionError, APITimeoutError, 5xx APIStatusError) and 401 token-refresh are NOT retried (reproduced: single APIConnectionError returns sentinel after 1 attempt). Full fix scheduled in W-16 (Sprint 28). Separately, the aice_rlm_planner_fallbacks_total metric is still not emitted.

Summary: [F-CC-R02] _synthesize fallback returns planner-shaped JSON to DA as if synthesized
Description: When synthesis LLM fails, _default_llm returns a planner JSON; DA receives it as code-gen context. CIA gets {'reasoning':'LLM unavailable',...} as code-gen context. Fix: distinguish purpose at _default_llm level; raise typed LLMUnavailableError on synthesis failure. See workstream W-09.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed (2026-06-19); RLMContext.degraded added and surfaced via to_dict/rlm_orchestrate; _synthesize returns (final, tokens, degraded) and no longer passes the LLM-unavailable sentinel off as content. Implemented via sentinel detection rather than a typed LLMUnavailableError to preserve the pluggable _llm_fn 3-arg contract used by DAs/tests.

Summary: [F-CC-R03] Greedy {[\s\S]*} JSON regex; query[:200] truncates complex queries
Description: Planner output parsing uses overly greedy regex; complex queries get silently truncated. Replace with brace-counting parser; pass full query. See workstream W-09.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed (2026-06-19); brace-counting _extract_json_object respects string literals, skips malformed/non-dict fragments and resumes at the next brace; planner fallback rebuilds from the full original query (no query[:200] truncation).

Summary: [F-CC-R04] _synthesize truncates each sub-query at 2000 chars; defeats RLM purpose
Description: Synthesis input has hardcoded per-step 2000-char cap, session_context 500-char cap. For code-gen contexts that easily exceed 5000 chars, ~60% of retrieved context is dropped. Replace with model-aware budget. See workstream W-09.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed (2026-06-19); model-aware synthesis budget (_MODEL_CONTEXT_TOKENS / _SYNTH_RESPONSE_TOKENS) replaces the hardcoded per-step char cap.

Summary: [F-CC-R05] RLM planning prompts hardcoded; per-task customization buried
Description: _PLAN_CONTEXT dict mixes 21 task types in one file. Move to planning_prompts.yaml. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-R06] RLM doesn't track per-step elapsed time in context; only totals
Description: SubQueryStep.elapsed_s captured but not aggregated for slow-step diagnosis. Add per-step Prometheus histogram. See workstream W-11.

Summary: [F-CC-R07] Planner can return >6 sub-queries despite max_steps prompt instruction
Description: LLM occasionally returns 7-8 steps. Validate and truncate. See workstream W-09.

Summary: [F-CC-R08] Sub-query alpha values not validated against [0,1] range
Description: LLM occasionally returns alpha=1.5 or negative. Clamp to [0,1]. See workstream W-09.

Summary: [F-CC-R09] RLM elapsed_s tracking uses time.time() not time.monotonic()
Description: Wall-clock changes (NTP) cause negative durations. Use monotonic. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-R10] Sub-query trace not persisted to PostgreSQL for analysis
Description: Per-step trace lost at session_end. Persist to response_archive for productivity analytics. See workstream W-11.

Summary: [F-CC-R11] Planning prompt for adhoc task_type missing
Description: When task_type=='adhoc', no specific planning prompt; fallback to generic. Add. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-C01] ContextBuilder slot budget mutation in earlier code (already fixed)
Description: Confirmed fixed via copy.deepcopy. Verification only. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-C02] 10-slot budget allocation doesn't account for token estimation drift
Description: Slots assume avg ~800 tokens; actual drift means some slots are 30% under-utilized. Adaptive sizing. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-C03] ContextBuilder doesn't dedupe results across slots
Description: Same Function node retrieved for both 'API signature' slot and 'usage' slot wastes budget. Dedupe by node id. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-C04] Slot budget vs total_budget reconciliation
Description: Sum of per-slot budgets can exceed total_budget if all slots packed. Add cap. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-C05] ContextBuilder formats results as Markdown tables; some DAs need plain text
Description: Markdown formatting confuses some DAs' LLM prompts. Add format= parameter. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-C06] MemoryLayer ContextBuilder import path inconsistent with HybridRAG
Description: Imports as 'src.MemoryLayer.memory.context_builder' in some files, 'MemoryLayer.context_builder' in others. Reconcile in W-18. See workstream W-18.

Summary: [F-CC-K01] _fuzzy_find Cypher label injection in knowledge_intelligence.py
Description: f'MATCH (n:{label})' interpolates label without sanitization. REV1-H17 fixed graph_search.py and services.py; never reached knowledge_intelligence.py. Currently labels are hardcoded so not exploitable today; defense-in-depth via shared _kg_safety.py. See workstream W-04.

Summary: [F-CC-K02] _run_cypher missing access_mode='READ'
Description: All ~10 KI tools (bulk of AICE read traffic) bypass read-replica routing. Add access_mode=neo4j.READ_ACCESS. CI gate TestREV1H14_ReadAccessMode catches. See workstream W-10.

Summary: [F-CC-K03] find_callers query has no LIMIT; can return 10K+ rows for hot functions
Description: MATCH (f)-[:CALLS_INTERNALLY]->(target) without LIMIT. Add config-driven cap. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-K04] find_requirement_traces doesn't filter by ASIL
Description: Returns all traces; ASIL filtering is downstream consumer responsibility. Add asil_filter parameter. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CC-K05] query_dependencies hardcodes max_depth=3; should be parameter
Description: Limits debug analysis. Make parameter. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B01] --clear deletes entire database despite module-scoped builder
Description: python build_knowledge_graph.py --module ADC --clear runs MATCH (n) DETACH DELETE n — deletes ALL data. No prompt, no audit log. Single-command total-data-loss footgun. Fix: --clear becomes module-scoped; --clear-all requires interactive confirmation + audit row. See workstream W-03.

Summary: [F-CD-B02] _create_constraints, _create_nodes, _create_edges f-string label/property interpolation
Description: Same Cypher injection pattern as F-CC-K01. Apply shared _kg_safety.py. See workstream W-04.

Summary: [F-CD-B03] UNWIND batch poisoned by one bad item; entire batch rolled back
Description: Single ConstraintViolation aborts 500-item batch. Job reports '0 nodes created' for batch without saying which item caused failure. Add per-item validation pre-batching + per-batch failure recovery via batch-size-1 retry. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CD-B04] _create_edges does MATCH (a {id: ...}) without label
Description: Full-database scan per match instead of label-indexed lookup. ~30-60s per ingestion of useless work. Add label-aware MATCH. Combined with W-17. See workstream W-17.

Summary: [F-CD-B05] _create_constraints doesn't preserve existing user-added indexes
Description: Re-running constraint creation drops user-added indexes. Filter to AICE-managed only. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B06] Sum mode dependency_fetcher doesn't respect HTTP retries on rate-limit
Description: Fetcher hits Bitbucket without backoff. Use shared retry helper from W-16. See workstream W-16.

Summary: [F-CD-B07] _connect() raises sys.exit(1) on init failure (kills MCP)
Description: build_knowledge_graph imported as module from MCP causes whole-server exit. Raise exception instead. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B08] Module name in Sum mode hardcoded to 'AS460_TC499N_STD_*'
Description: Default config list assumes one specific silicon. Make configurable. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B09] auto_stub_generator import lazy; failures silent
Description: If stub generator missing, falls through to existing stubs without warning. Surface. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B10] build_knowledge_graph CLI doesn't accept --workspace
Description: Inferred from module name. Make explicit for clarity. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B11] Build progress logging at INFO drowns useful messages
Description: Per-batch INFO logs at 500-batch granularity = thousands of lines. Demote to DEBUG. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-B12] cp1252→UTF-8 byte corruption in build_knowledge_graph.py
Description: ΓåÆ ΓÇª Γèó characters in source. Single sed pass per Pass 4b grep gate G-04. See workstream W-23.

Summary: [F-CD-B13] Confidence calculator output not persisted with batch result
Description: Quality scoring done but not saved with the batch summary. Save. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-I01] _merge_nodes/_merge_edges Cypher injection
Description: Same pattern as F-CC-K01, F-CD-B02. Apply _kg_safety.py. See workstream W-04.

Summary: [F-CD-I02] ingest_swa silently picks parser-detected vs pipeline-passed module
Description: Inconsistent: F-CD-I03 says always uses self.module; need to verify. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-I03] ingest_swa always uses self.module over parser-detected
Description: If pipeline param is wrong, every function gets wrong module label, polluting the namespace. Add module-mismatch warning. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-I04] ingest_sfr doesn't validate register_name uniqueness within module
Description: Same register name across modules collides. Add module prefix. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-I05] create_cross_source_relationships requires manual trigger after all ingest_* calls
Description: Easy to forget. Auto-call at end of each builder.run(). See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-I06] BATCH_SIZE = 500 hardcoded; not configurable per environment
Description: Small Neo4j servers OOM. Move to config. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-I07] _serialize_complex JSON-encodes lists/dicts but doesn't verify post-serialization size
Description: Large struct member lists exceed Neo4j property size limit silently. Cap. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CD-Q01] _fetch f-string rel-type injection in query_knowledge_graph.py (Legacy file)
Description: Same Cypher injection pattern. Apply _kg_safety.py. See workstream W-04.

Summary: [F-CD-Q02] find_path takes max_depth as int but doesn't bound it
Description: Caller passes max_depth=100 → query times out. Bound to 1..10. See workstream W-04.

Summary: [F-CD-Q03] find_orphan_requirements label-list interpolation
Description: Same pattern. Apply _kg_safety.py. See workstream W-04.

Summary: [F-CD-X01] ILLDKnowledgeGraphBuilder + ILLDKGBuilder coexist with different ID formats
Description: Two ILLD builders. KGBuilder writes id='IfxCan_init'; KGBuilder writes id='FUNC_IfxCan_init'. Same logical function ingested twice with different IDs. Search returns duplicates. See workstream W-17.

Summary: [F-CD-X02] Three Neo4j-write retry implementations across MCAL/ILLD/batch_ingestion
Description: Pattern #2 — fix once via shared retry helper. batch_ingestion.Neo4jBatchWriter does no retry, just logs and moves on. ~500 nodes silently dropped per 50K ingestion at 1% transient failure rate. See workstream W-16.

Summary: [F-CE-C01] _find_libclang_dll only checks .dll for LLVM_HOME fallback (Linux silently uses regex)
Description: Linux developers with LLVM_HOME set silently get regex parser; KG fidelity drops invisibly. config_struct_resolver has different libclang detection. Unify; OS-aware extension; aice_c_parser_clang_available gauge. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-C02] Clang fallback to regex logged at DEBUG; output dict has no parser_method field
Description: Operators don't see fallback. Promote to WARNING. Add parser_method='clang'|'regex' to output. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-C03] Clang diagnostics collection has no severity threshold; warnings spam KG
Description: All severities (notes through fatals) stored in result dict. Filter to severity>=3 by default. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-C04] _extract_internal_calls doesn't dedup; same callee N times → N MERGE operations
Description: If Adc_Init calls Mcal_GetMode 5 times, 5 MERGE operations though MERGE is idempotent. Dedup at extraction. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-S01] illd_swa_parser LLM enrichment has different retry shape from pdf_pipeline
Description: Two LLM-using parsers, two distinct retry impls. Pattern #2. Consolidate via shared retry. See workstream W-16.

Summary: [F-CE-S02] _section regex fragile to comment style variations
Description: Banner regex matches /*-- X --*/ only; if iLLD changes banner style, parser silently parses entire file as section. Try multiple patterns. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-S03] Module = self._base.replace('_swa','').replace('Ifx','') — order-sensitive, not anchored
Description: MyIfxModule.h has Ifx stripped from middle. Use re.sub with prefix/suffix anchors. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-S04] LLM model defaults differ across files (gpt-4o, gpt-5.2)
Description: Three different defaults across pdf_parser, pdf_pipeline, RLM, illd_swa_parser docstring. Consolidate via AICE_DEFAULT_LLM_MODEL env. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-P01] pdf_parser._BATCH_SIZE=2 hardcoded; pdf_pipeline.py has adaptive DPI — dual implementations
Description: Two PDF code paths with different design decisions. Pattern #3. Consolidate. See workstream W-17.

Summary: [F-CE-P02] pdf_pipeline retry logic correct but tangled in 60-line function
Description: Hard to test in isolation. Extract LLMRetryClient wrapper. Combined with W-16. See workstream W-16.

Summary: [F-CE-P03] _page_has_figure body not visible; potential perf issue at scale
Description: 500 sequential calls for 500-page PDF. Verify; parallelize if needed. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-A01] _strip_template_macros blindly strips EB tresos macros without XML structure validation
Description: Stripping can leave duplicated containers, unmatched tags. ET.fromstring may succeed with wrong content. Validate post-strip; warn if structurally ambiguous. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-A02] _extract_param_type fallback returns raw tag, polluting KG
Description: Caller gets param_type='ECUC-NUMERICAL-PARAM-VALUE' instead of 'NUMERICAL'. Skip entry; don't insert with raw tag. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-A03] ARXML namespace _NS hardcoded to r4.0; R4.4 ARXML files silently produce 0 modules
Description: findall(f'{_NS}AR-PACKAGES') only matches r4.0. Detect schema dynamically. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-T01] testspec_parsers opens 50MB+ xlsx with read_only=False; ~500MB peak RAM
Description: 20-module batch ingestion → repeated 500MB peaks. K8s pod with 2GB limit risks OOM-kill. Profile, cap workbook size, hybrid mode. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-T02] _PRQ_REF_RE uses re.IGNORECASE → case-sensitive Jama IDs become orphan refs
Description: Lowercase test-spec author mistake propagates to KG, references node that doesn't exist. Strip IGNORECASE or normalize to upper. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-T03] _extract_feature_id_from_syntax relaxed-GUID fallback too permissive
Description: Truncated/OCR-corrupted GUID matches accept invalid IDs. Flag with confidence='low'. See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-T04] _parse_spec_table_rows resilient to PDF page-break headings (defensive parsing for upstream issue)
Description: Couples parser to pdf_pipeline output style. Fix at source (strip page-breaks in pdf_pipeline). See workstream W-20.

Summary: [F-CE-O01] OCR subprocess accepts arbitrary image_path from public method
Description: OCRProcessor.process_page_image is public; image_path validation missing. OCR_LANGUAGE env unvalidated. Validate path containment + language regex. See workstream W-05.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed (2026-06-19); process_page_image validates OCR language regex and contains image via safe_path_under (OCR_ALLOWED_ROOTS; system temp included for pdftoppm output).

Summary: [F-CE-O02] _estimate_confidence body not visible; may return constant
Description: If returns 0.5 constant, downstream consumers think they have OCR quality scoring when they don't. Verify. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-X01] Output shape inconsistency: 7 different return-type contracts across 14 parsers
Description: arxml_parser returns dict; pdf_parser returns list[str] (or str per docstring); xlsx_parser returns sheet→rows; testspec returns node-type→list. Define ParserResult Protocol; standardize. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-X02] illd_swa_parser (C headers) vs swa_parsers (markdown) — same prefix, opposite scope
Description: Pattern #3. Rename illd_swa_parser → swa_header_parser; swa_parsers → swa_markdown_parser. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-X03] Parser parse(path, ...) signatures diverge despite uniform docstring
Description: c_parser has 6 params, pdf_parser has 8, illd_swa has 5. Use **opts pattern. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CE-X04] Parsers/__init__.py __all__ declares 11 of 14 actual parsers
Description: regdef_parser, swa_parsers, swud_parsers, testspec_parsers, ocr_processor missing from __all__. Add. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CF-X01] verify_ssl=False hardcoded in dependency_fetcher and header_fetcher Bitbucket callers
Description: Both pass IFX_USERNAME/IFX_PASSWORD plus pull iLLD source code with TLS validation explicitly disabled. Highest single-finding security severity. Use Infineon CA bundle. See workstream W-06.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): W-06 implemented 2026-06-19: new src/_common/tls_config.py (get_verify_setting secure-by-default + enforce_tls_policy guard); header_fetcher/dependency_fetcher/rc1_dependency_fetcher use get_verify_setting(); Bitbucket/Jama/Polarion __init__ refuse verify_ssl=False without AICE_ALLOW_INSECURE_TLS=1. 245 connector+TLS tests pass.

Summary: [F-CF-X02] Credentials stored as plaintext on instance attributes; no zeroing on close
Description: Memory dumps expose all service tokens. EU AI Act / ASPICE audit liability. Add _SecretStr wrapper across all 4 connectors. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-X03] Four duplicate retry implementations (Jama/Polarion/Jenkins/Bitbucket)
Description: Pattern #2. Consolidate via shared retry helper from W-16. See workstream W-16.

Summary: [F-CF-X04] Sync-state JSON files written to user-controlled path without containment
Description: If a future MCP tool exposes sync_state_dir as a parameter, path traversal becomes exploitable. Add allowlisted root validation. See workstream W-05.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Closed (2026-06-19); JamaConnector and PolarionConnector contain sync_state_dir via safe_path_under before mkdir (AICE_SYNC_STATE_ROOTS override).

Summary: [F-CF-X05] httpx.Client created once at construction; cannot pick up CA rotation
Description: Long-lived MCP server processes (weeks) can't recover from quarterly CA rotations. Add _recreate_client() on TLS errors. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-J01] Jama get_project_id walks entire project list every call
Description: 20-module ingestion = 20× full pagination. Hits Jama rate limiter. Add 5-min TTL cache. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-J02] JamaItem.from_api_dict silently coerces missing fields to id=-1 sentinel
Description: Sentinel propagates into KG as orphan reference. Fail loud on missing id. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-J03] JamaConnector docstrings use unicode em-dashes (cosmetic; legacy terminals render '?')
Description: Cosmetic; not in same byte-corruption class as F-CD-B12. No action. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CF-P01] Polarion JWT captured at construction; never refreshed
Description: After token expiration (1-24h), every call returns 401; PolarionAuthError is non-retryable. Multi-day MCP uptime → silent sync failure. Token-provider callable instead of static token. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-P02] validate_connection uses hardcoded AURIX_RC1_MCAL project ID
Description: If Polarion project renamed, validate_connection 404s and looks like auth error. Make env-configurable. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-P03] incremental_sync FAILED status not differentiated from transient failure
Description: Single network blip marks sync as FAILED forever. Add TRANSIENT_FAILURE status with auto-recovery. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-P04] PolarionTestEnvironment.from_api_dict has 13 fields with .get(k,'') or '' boilerplate
Description: Repetitive defensive code. Helper _str(d, k) pattern. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-CF-N01] JenkinsConnector has 5 except (NoBuildData, Exception): pass blocks
Description: Absorbs JenkinsConnectionError silently. Partial JobInfo population looks like 'no builds yet.' Replace with explicit exception types. See workstream W-16.

Summary: [F-CF-N02] JenkinsConnector connect() except Exception wraps everything as ConnectionError
Description: TLS errors, config errors, library bugs all become 'Failed to connect.' Distinguish JenkinsTLSError, JenkinsConfigError. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-B01] Bitbucket get_files_bulk silently drops failures
Description: Returns dict with successes only; no way to know which files failed. Return BulkFetchResult(files, failures). See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-B02] Bitbucket path normalized via lstrip('/') only; no traversal protection
Description: Defense-in-depth gap. Add explicit ../  and character validation. See workstream W-21.

Summary: [F-CF-B03] from_clone_url documented but body not visible; verify presence
Description: Possible doc/code drift. Verify, fix or remove from docs. See workstream W-25.

Summary: [F-P5-D01] 15 of 21 DAs lack API key provisioning in api_keys.yaml
Description: Only 6 DAs (gest, cia, reva, acra, saga, triplea) have explicit api_keys; 15 unprovisioned. The '21 DAs served' claim is currently 6 served + 15 unaccounted-for. Provision keys, mark PLANNED, or document SHARED arrangement. See workstream W-15.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): Provisioned in mcp/auth/api_keys.yaml: 16 canonical AI Domain Assistants (MCAL-only) + EDA (Embedded Driver Assistant; iLLD public+developer, no MCAL) + 2 admin = 19 keys. iLLD reserved for EDA. 2026-06-19 per stakeholder direction: removed 8 codebase-only assistants (cia,cta,saga,page,kw,voltai,rma) not in Confluence; invented mira removed; stoptyping not provisioned.

Summary: [F-P5-D02] RLM DA_TASK_MAPPING codes don't match AI_USAGE_POLICY.md codes
Description: SASA vs none; DaFaA vs DFA; HazopA vs HZOP; PRQ_Drafter vs PRQ. Unify on docs-side codes. See workstream W-15.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): CORRECTED 2026-06-19: AI_USAGE_POLICY.md does not exist; authoritative codes are the Confluence MCSWAI 'AI Assistants' names. DA_TASK_MAPPING + descriptions + api_keys now use SASA/DaFaA/HAZOPA/PRQGEN (earlier SAAN/DFA/HZOP/PRQ was wrong) + added canonical SWQMA/J-WIZ/Zephyr; removed invented mira. 2026-06-19 per stakeholder direction: removed 8 code-only DAs (CIA/CTA/SAGA/PAGE/KW/VoltAI/RMA/StopTyping) from DA_TASK_MAPPING + added EDA->code_generation.

Summary: [F-P5-D03] StopTyping task type has no DA owner or use case
Description: DA_TASK_MAPPING has 'StopTyping' but no DA matches. Decide if it's a DA at all; document or remove. See workstream W-15.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): W-15 2026-06-19: kept StopTyping (real stop_typing/quick-lookup task) and documented as a shared utility, not a provisioned DA persona (no api_keys entry).

Summary: [F-P5-I01] Cat 5 (Ingestion) is admin-only, blocking KW (Knowledge Weaver)
Description: KW's task is knowledge_ingestion but ingestion tools are admin-tier. Decide DA vs pipeline for KW. If DA, expose developer-tier ingest_session_attachment. If pipeline, remove from DA list. See workstream W-15.

Summary: [F-P5-I02] Tier mismatches: SAGA/TripleA at developer; CIA/ACRA at public
Description: Tier assignments under-justified. CIA might benefit from execute_cypher access. Revisit per DA. See workstream W-15.

Summary: [F-P5-I03] No aice-da-sdk Python package; each DA reinvents the 6-step lifecycle
Description: AICE-DA-004 says CIA is the reference impl; no other DA has reference impl. New DA = 1 sprint integration. SDK eliminates repeated work. See workstream W-19.

Summary: [F-P5-M01] No da_name label on Prometheus metrics
Description: Without da_name, no per-DA productivity analysis possible. Add da_name + tier labels via context vars. See workstream W-11.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): W-11 2026-06-19: tier label added to aice_tool_requests_total (_resolve_da_tier + _current_da_tier set in _authorize); NEW aice_da_* catalog (session_duration/outcomes/context_tokens/first_result_latency/pattern_hits/llm_tokens, 6 metrics + NoOp stubs) emitted at session_end/build_context/submit_human_feedback/evaluate_confidence/rlm_orchestrate/get_failure_patterns; wired into aice-da-productivity dashboard (14 panels). Label da_name used (not plan's da).

Summary: [F-P5-M02] task_type='unknown' will accumulate without resolution
Description: Sessionless calls have no task_type; metrics get noisy. Make mandatory at session_start; default to 'adhoc' for sessionless. See workstream W-11.
Status Notes (2026-06-19 branch audit): W-11 2026-06-19: session_start gained task_type param (default 'adhoc'), stored in session (_task_type) + retrieved via _session_task_type() to label all aice_da_* series. Never 'unknown' now.
